sections.paragraph: δεῖ [impersonal verb from δέω ‘lack, stand in need of’] the basic idea is that circumstances or conditions, expressed or implied, determine or set expectations for an outcome, event, or performance: ‘it is necessary’, one must Mt 16:21; Ac 1:16; Tit 1:11; Rv 1:1; one needs, as need requires Ro 8:26; one ought w. focus on appropriateness Mt 18:33; Ro 12:3; 1 Th 4:1; 2 Pt 3:11.—δέον (nt. ptc.) ἐστίν: it is appropriate Ac 19:36; w. εἰ if it must be = if conditions are such 1 Pt 1:6. In ref. to the past ἔδει: had to Lk 15:32; should have Ac 24:19; 2 Cor 2:3.
